answer.text:
## Short answer

For a **club-deal PE fund**, the **Jersey Private Fund (JPF)** is the default Jersey regime. Club deals — a small group of professional / institutional co-investors backing a defined opportunity — are explicitly called out as a textbook JPF use case (`jersey/funds/jersey-private-fund.md`). Reach for an **Expert Fund** only if you expect to push past the 50-investor cap or need wider marketing latitude.

## Why JPF fits a club deal

- **Investor cap aligns naturally.** A JPF accepts up to **50 offers / investors**, all professional or "eligible" — i.e. exactly the shape of a club. (`jersey/funds/jersey-private-fund.md`)
- **Fast and light.** JFSC authorisation typically issues within **~48 hours**; the regime is "light-touch" with a Jersey-licensed administrator acting as the **designated service provider** (DSP) instead of direct fund-level JFSC supervision. (`jersey/funds/jersey-private-fund.md`)
- **PPM, not prospectus.** Disclosure is a Private Placement Memorandum plus eligible-investor self-certification — costs and timeline fit closed-ended PE economics. (`jersey/use-cases/fund-counsel/choosing-fund-regime.md`)
- **Vehicle flexibility.** JPF can be a Jersey LP (most common for PE), a no-par-value company, a unit trust, or a cell company. (`jersey/funds/jersey-private-fund.md`)

## When to flip to Expert Fund instead

The Expert Fund regime has **no specific investor cap** (subject to each investor meeting "expert" criteria — typically US$100k minimum subscription, net-assets threshold, or institutional status) and tolerates **wider private placement marketing**, including AIFMD NPPR positioning into EU member states. The trade-off: more detailed offering memorandum, days-to-weeks authorisation rather than 48 hours, and a fully FSB-licensed service provider stack. (`jersey/funds/expert-fund.md`, `jersey/use-cases/fund-counsel/choosing-fund-regime.md`)

Concretely, prefer Expert Fund if any of the following is true:

- Investor count is expected to materially exceed 50.
- Your distribution model is broader private placement to expert investors across multiple jurisdictions, rather than a known closed club.
- You need AIFMD NPPR marketing into the EU as a structural requirement.

## Decision summary for your facts

A **club deal** is, by definition, a bounded set of known co-investors on a single deal or programme — the JPF was built for this. Unless you're planning to scale the investor list beyond ~50, or to actively market beyond the club, **JPF is the right call**. (`jersey/use-cases/fund-counsel/hedge-fund-pe-vehicles.md` calls out "club deals, single-LP" expressly among the PE/VC use cases that fit the JPF.)

## Caveats on the sources

All four corpus files I cited (`jersey-private-fund.md`, `expert-fund.md`, `choosing-fund-regime.md`, `hedge-fund-pe-vehicles.md`) carry **status: draft** with `last_verified: 2026-05-15`. Freshness is fine (4 days old), but the corpus's editorial team has not yet verified these files to `stable`. Re-check the JPF rules against the **JFSC Jersey Private Fund Guide** and the Expert-Fund criteria against the **JFSC Jersey Expert Fund Guide** before filing.

## Files cited

1. `jersey/funds/jersey-private-fund.md` (draft, last_verified 2026-05-15)
2. `jersey/funds/expert-fund.md` (draft, last_verified 2026-05-15)
3. `jersey/use-cases/fund-counsel/choosing-fund-regime.md` (draft, last_verified 2026-05-15)
4. `jersey/use-cases/fund-counsel/hedge-fund-pe-vehicles.md` (draft, last_verified 2026-05-15)

> This is information drawn from the offshoreai corpus, not legal, tax, or investment advice. Verify the cited primary sources before acting.
